creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Yen-Ming, good day.

Sun, Sep 28, 2008 at 09:15:44AM -0700, Yen-Ming Lee wrote:
> I'm not sure what happened in your environment, but on my 7.0-RELEASE
> box, define BATCH=yes will trigger PERL_MM_USE_DEFAULT="YES", and then
> pass that prompt with default answer as expected.

Ah, OK: I was not aware of PERL_MM_USE_DEFAULT, so I just patched
the port without prior checking of the result of setting BATCH.

Sorry for the noise, PR can be closed as invalid.
-- 
Eygene

 \`.|\..----...-'`   `-._.-'_.-'`   #  Remember that it is hard
 /  ' `         ,       __.--'      #  to read the on-line manual   
 )/' _/     \   `-_,   /            #  while single-stepping the kernel.
 `-'" `"\_  ,_.-;_.-\_ ',  fsc/as   #
     _.-'_./   {_.'   ; /           #    -- FreeBSD Developers handbook 
    {_.-``-'         {_/            #
